name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram is a money transfer service offering domestic and international money sending/receiving at physical locations across the U.S., including partner retailers and standalone agents.
description_long:
MoneyGram is an established money transfer company that provides financial services through a network of physical locations and agent partners. The company operates by allowing consumers to send and receive money both domestically and internationally, with a focus on accessibility through multiple retail partners rather than a standalone branch network.

MoneyGram's core service offering includes money transfers (sending and receiving), check cashing, money orders, and wire transfers. Based on the Memphis location page, the company operates through a network of retail agent locations, including partnerships with convenience stores, check cashing outlets like Ace Cash Express, and pharmacy chains like CVS. Customers can initiate transfers both online and in-person at these locations, providing flexibility for consumers who prefer digital or face-to-face transactions.

What distinguishes MoneyGram from traditional banks is its focus on accessibility and speed rather than comprehensive financial services. The company operates extended hours through retail partners (some locations open as early as 7:00 AM and stay open until 11:59 PM), making services available when traditional banks are closed. The distributed agent model means consumers can access MoneyGram at hundreds of neighborhood locations rather than traveling to a specific branch.

However, MoneyGram should be evaluated as a transactional service provider rather than a financial institution offering savings accounts, loans, or credit products. Fees for money transfer services are typically higher than traditional bank wire transfers, making the service most cost-effective for consumers prioritizing speed and convenience over minimal costs. The service is most relevant for consumers without traditional banking access, those needing rapid fund transfers, and immigrants sending money internationally.
pros: 48+ locations in Memphis alone indicating widespread accessibility and convenience
Extended operating hours through retail partners (some open until 11:59 PM) vs. traditional bank hours
Hybrid service model allowing both online and offline transaction initiation
Multiple agent partner types (Ace Cash Express, CVS, convenience stores) provide varied location options
Both domestic and international money transfer capabilities from single service provider
Fast transaction processing enabling same-day fund delivery in many cases
cons: Money transfer fees are significantly higher than traditional bank wire transfers
Limited to transactional services—no savings accounts, credit products, or financial planning
Dependent on third-party retail locations, so service quality and availability varies by agent
International transfers subject to exchange rate markups that reduce recipient value
No account features or record-keeping tools beyond individual transaction history
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked consumers without access to traditional bank accounts
Immigrants and expatriates sending money to family members internationally
Consumers needing rapid fund transfers outside standard business hours
People requiring check cashing or money order services at convenient neighborhood locations
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for unbanked consumers and those sending international remittances who prioritize speed and convenience over cost. The main caveat is that transfer fees are substantially higher than traditional banking alternatives, making this service most economical for time-sensitive transfers rather than regular money movement.
